description: Lock electrical_appliances_early to early electric resistance-heating devices, excluding motor appliances and central-station infrastructure while preserving the documented lighting-circuit connection.

checks.reason: The narrowed node remains anchored to Seely's electric flat-iron patent issued on 6 June 1882.
Electric current is the intrinsic energy input for every resistance-heating appliance inside the locked scope.
Seely's preferred lamp-socket connection used an existing lighting circuit, but the patent allowed other suitable electricity sources.
Central-station generation is a possible deployment context, not part of the resistance-heating appliance mechanism.
Motor-driven appliances are explicitly outside the resistance-heating scope.
